task.title: Temporarily configuring a device as unmanaged in NetworkManager

context.p: You can temporarily configure devices as unmanaged, for example, for testing purposes. This change persists a reload and restart of the NetworkManager systemd service, but not a system reboot.

step.cmd: Optional: Display the list of devices to identify the device you want to set as unmanaged:

info.codeblock: # nmcli device status DEVICE TYPE STATE CONNECTION enp1s0 ethernet disconnected -- ...

step.cmd: Set the enp1s0 device to the unmanaged state:

info.codeblock: # nmcli device set enp1s0 managed no

li.codeblock: # nmcli device status DEVICE TYPE STATE CONNECTION enp1s0 ethernet unmanaged -- ...
li.p: The unmanaged state next to the enp1s0 device indicates that NetworkManager does not manage this device.
